metadata.name: PolitiFact
metadata.description: Real political statements from PolitiFact with 6-level verdicts

samples.claim: Says Hillary Clinton has donated every cent shes ever earned from speaking fees to charity.
samples.label: false

samples.claim: Says the secret John Doe criminal investigation of his campaign has been resolved and two judges have said it is over.
samples.label: false

samples.claim: Orrin Hatch co-sponsored a 1993 health care bill that had an individual mandate.
samples.label: true

samples.claim: Mark Herring voted to allow sex offenders, including rapists, to serve shorter sentences.
samples.label: barely-true

samples.claim: In many instances, (peoples) health care costs are more than their mortgage costs or their rent, which, by the way, is a first in American history.
samples.label: barely-true

samples.claim: In countries where there are higher, more strict gun laws, there is more gun violence.
samples.label: false

samples.claim: Unfortunately, since Mr. Fosters been mayor ... there are less people employed in the city of St. Petersburg than there were prior to his taking office.
samples.label: false

samples.claim: "There's only four senators senior to me ... there's still 44 older than me."
samples.label: mostly-true

samples.claim: The words subhuman mongrel, which Ted Nugent called President Barack Obama, were used by the Nazis to justify the genocide of the Jewish community.
samples.label: true

samples.claim: Marijuana is less toxic than alcohol.
samples.label: mostly-true

samples.claim: Says Donald Trump has changed his mind on abortion.
samples.label: true

samples.claim: A new ruling by the EPA would force dairy farmers to comply with the Spill Prevention, Control and Countermeasure Program when dealing with spilt milk -- the same regulations oil and natural gas producers must follow.
samples.label: false

samples.claim: Missouri is #1 in the nation for holding down tuition increases at public universities.
samples.label: mostly-true

samples.claim: Says 95 percent of people caught crossing the U.S.-Mexico border said in a survey we are coming because weve been promised amnesty.
samples.label: half-true

samples.claim: When undocumented children are picked up at the border and told to appear later in court ... 90 percent do not then show up.
samples.label: false

samples.claim: John McCain "has threatened extinction for North Korea and sung songs about bombing Iran."
samples.label: half-true

samples.claim: In the last 10 years, Georgias middle class income has dropped $6,500.
samples.label: true

samples.claim: Oil production is down 14 percent this year on federal land.
samples.label: half-true

samples.claim: Onion Creeks highest flow rate on a recent night of flooding was 120,000 cubic feet per second, which is nearly double the average flow rate of Niagara Falls.
samples.label: barely-true

samples.claim: U.S. Rep. Jim Langevin didn't want a border fence to block illegal immigration "because he is afraid that someone will get hurt trying to go around the fence."
samples.label: pants-fire

samples.claim: Says he brought 1,200 jobs to Texas by moving his factories here from China.
samples.label: half-true

samples.claim: Says a company that got tax breaks from the state later laid off 100 Ohioans, even as its CEO cut a half-million-dollar check to Kasichs super PAC.
samples.label: mostly-true

samples.claim: Says the oil industry subsidies that President Barack Obama is attacking dont exist
samples.label: false

samples.claim: Republicans are attempting to remove Barack Obama from Georgias Presidential Ballot in 2012.
samples.label: half-true

samples.claim: Federal spending on entitlements is projected to consume all revenue by 2045.
samples.label: mostly-true

samples.claim: About two-thirds of Medicaid beneficiaries are children, but they account for one-third of the programs cost, while one-third are elderly, and they account for two-thirds of the cost.
samples.label: barely-true

samples.claim: Fox News is banned in Canada because it violatesa law thatprevents news channels from lying to their viewers.
samples.label: pants-fire

samples.claim: Says Tom Cottons vote in Congress to change Medicare will increase out-of-pocket expenses for every senior in Arkansas.
samples.label: barely-true

samples.claim: Says Hillary Clinton called the Trans-Pacific Partnership the gold standard. You called it the gold standard of trade deals. You said its the finest deal youve ever seen.
samples.label: mostly-true

samples.claim: Today California is kicking our butt, creating more jobs and more economic growth than Texas.
samples.label: true

samples.claim: Says a rather extraordinary amount of non-classroom employees were added by Texas school districts over the last decade.
samples.label: mostly-true

samples.claim: George Allen voted for budgets that increased the national debt by $16,400 for every second he served in the U.S. Senate.
samples.label: true

samples.claim: President Obama has the lowest public approval ratings of any president in modern times.
samples.label: pants-fire

samples.claim: I can tell you that the enhanced interrogation techniques that have since been banned by this administration specifically waterboarding work.
samples.label: false

samples.claim: In the Illinois Legislature, Barack Obama "voted 'present,' instead of yes or no" on seven votes involving abortion rights.
samples.label: true

samples.claim: "The White House is won in the swing states, and I am winning the swing states."
samples.label: barely-true

samples.claim: The Democrat-backed health care reform plan "will require (Americans) to subsidize abortion with their hard-earned tax dollars."
samples.label: false

samples.claim: Says kids who start in the ninth grade in the city of Newark this past September, 23 percent of them will graduate in four years.
samples.label: pants-fire

samples.claim: Studies have shown that in the absence of federal reproductive health funds, we are going to see the level of abortion in Georgia increase by about 44 percent.
samples.label: half-true

samples.claim: Says Rick Perry recently said he wanted another (Texas) business tax.
samples.label: pants-fire

samples.claim: Foreign Chinese prostitution money is allegedly behind the groups funding Congressman Sean Duffys Republican Majority.
samples.label: pants-fire

samples.claim: Of the 20 bills [Rep. David] Cicilline has sponsored in his three years in office, not a single one has advanced past the embryonic committee stage.
samples.label: half-true

samples.claim: Says Chris Christies plan to kick-start our economy is to propose an income tax cut that disproportionately benefits the wealthy, and...hes still proposing it.
samples.label: pants-fire

samples.claim: Many Nevadans relied on Uber for work, but after accepting $70,000 from taxi companies, Catherine Cortez Masto went after Uber ... (driving) them out of town.
samples.label: barely-true

samples.claim: Says JimRenacci has consistently voted for loopholes that encourage outsourcing of our jobs.
samples.label: half-true

samples.claim: Says Chris Koster fell silentas attorney general in pursuing a case against a free classified service accused of promoting prostitution after accepting over $12,000 in campaign contributions from people affiliated with the service.
samples.label: barely-true

samples.claim: Wisconsin dropped from second in the country to 41st among states where more than half the students took the ACT exam.
samples.label: pants-fire

samples.claim: Two months ago, Donald Trump said that ISIS was not our fight.
samples.label: mostly-true

samples.claim: Under this tax cut, middle-class families dont save enough for a weeks worth of groceries, while millionaires save enough to go on an exotic vacation.
samples.label: true

samples.claim: Pre-existing conditions are covered under my (health care) plan.
samples.label: barely-true
